references: AIFMD Article 110 — Investor reporting and Annex IV
ESMA Technical Guidance v1.7 — Annex IV reporting field definitions
CSSF Regulation 10-04 — Organisational requirements for dual ManCos
reporting_scope: All AIFs must provide Annex IV transparency reports covering fund identity, exposures, leverage, liquidity profile, and related risks

leverage_calculation_methodology.notes: Leverage calculation methodology is standardised; fund strategy determines which method is primary

investor_concentration_reporting.requirements: Annex IV requires disclosure of investor concentration information including top beneficial owners, retail/professional investor breakdown, and concentration metrics relevant to fund liquidity and redemption risk
investor_concentration_reporting.regulatory_basis: AIFMD Article 110 + Annex IV template (Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 231/2013)
investor_concentration_reporting.notes: Specific investor concentration thresholds (e.g., 20% single investor, 50% top-3) are fund-level internal monitoring policies, not regulatory thresholds. These are documented in individual fund risk_policy.json files where applicable.

liquidity_reporting.requirement: Fund must disclose liquidity profile, redemption frequency, notice period
liquidity_reporting.lmt_tools: AIFMD II Article 16a — suspension, side pockets, capital call facility (if applicable)

unfunded_commitments.notes: Required disclosure in Annex IV for funds with unfunded commitments

principles_based_note: AIFMD does not prescribe one universal VaR model, confidence level, holding period, or concentration limit for all AIFs. Methodologies must be appropriate to the fund's strategy, risk profile, and liquidity profile.
